steps.title: Track the claim
steps.content: The claim says Newland arrives late because he enjoys anticipation. The quotation therefore has to do more than mention the opera or his lateness; it must show that thinking about a pleasure before it happens gives Newland special satisfaction.

steps.title: Eliminate unrelated details
steps.content: Choice A describes carriage etiquette, not Newland's feelings. Choice C reports that he arrived after the curtain rose, but it does not explain why. Choice D describes the beauty of the opera setting. The only remaining quotation directly says that thinking over a pleasure to come gave Newland subtler satisfaction than realizing it. The answer is B.
